steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content:
$$3x^2+17x-18x-28+16=3x^2-x-12$$
The key move is distributing the subtraction across every term of $g(x)$, which changes $+18x-16$ into $-18x+16$. After that, the $x$-terms combine to $-x$ and the constants combine to $-12$. The simplified expression matches the correct answer.
